knowledge_items.title: Reading Performance and Eye Movement Patterns in Children with ADHD: A Naturalistic Classroom Study
key_findings: Children with ADHD exhibit significantly more regressive saccades during reading compared to typically developing peers (p<0.001)
Fixation duration on connected text is 18% shorter in ADHD readers, correlating with lower reading comprehension scores
Eye movement abnormalities during reading correlate with teacher-reported inattention (r=0.54, p<0.001)
knowledge_items.methods_detail: Naturalistic classroom-based eye-tracking study of reading behavior in 164 children (82 ADHD, 82 controls), ages 8–12
limitations: Reading material difficulty not individually calibrated
